checks.a_analysis: All text elements in the JSON output, including headings and the primary contact paragraph, correspond directly to text present in the source PDF. The metadata title is an allowed AI-generated addition for accessibility purposes and is not considered a fabrication of body text.

The phone number (919-754-6949) and the mailing address (3514 Bush Street Raleigh, NC 27609) in the output match the source exactly. No fabricated contact details were introduced; the email is omitted rather than falsified.

The single page of the source document is accounted for in the output. The primary content (the flowchart) is represented as an image element with descriptive alt text, though its textual details are omitted (addressed in C5).
There are no tables in the source document; the content consists of text and flowcharts.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture a substantial portion of the document's information. Firstly, the contact email 'cjleadshelp@nc.gov' is missing from the primary contact paragraph. Secondly, the entire text content of the instructional flowchart—which constitutes the vast majority of the document's specific guidance—is omitted from the JSON text fields. While an AI-generated summary is provided in the alt text of the second image, it fails to include critical details such as 'Identifies & provides Signatory Authority', 'authorized security roles', the specific tasks of the ITS and CJLEADS Team in Step 3, and detailed training requirements. This represents an omission of over 80% of the document's instructional text content.
checks.b_evidence_source: cjleadshelp@nc.gov; Identifies & provides Signatory Authority; Identifies internal points of contact; Identifies authorized security roles; Files signed documents with CJLEADS Team; ITS and CJLEADS Team will: 1. Assist Agency with adding Org/Div/Sect 2. Agency Admin obtains Org NCID; NCID Administration; CJLEADS User Admin; (Accounts activated at the end of training class)
checks.b_evidence_output: The text content is entirely missing from the paragraph and section elements. The image alt text summary ('...steps including agency identification, license agreement, NCID registration, user administration, training...') omits these specific instructions.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main section, page 1, elements index 2 and 6

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: No significant wording changes that alter meaning were found in the extracted text. The main issues identified are significant omissions, which are covered under C5.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the output JSON matches the source PDF without any observed repetitions or duplications. Each section and image is represented exactly once in the sequence.

The main document title 'How Does My Agency Get CJLEADS?' is correctly identified as an H1. While subheadings within the flowchart (e.g., 'Step 1', 'Step 2') are not explicitly tagged as headings because they are encapsulated within the flowchart image, the primary heading structure provided in the text elements is logical and does not skip levels.
The source document does not contain any formal table structures. The flowchart is correctly identified as a graphic element.

The reading order in the output diverges from the visual hierarchy of the document. The 'Primary Contact' information, which is visually located in the mid-left area below 'Step 1' of the flowchart, is ordered as the second element (reading_order 2) immediately following the title. This causes the reading flow to jump past the Logo (reading_order 5) and the main flowchart (reading_order 6), which are visually superior on the page. However, since the information remains logically accessible, this is a minor structural fidelity issue.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual flow starts with Logo (top-left) and Heading (top-center), followed by the Flowchart (center). Contact info is located under Step 1 of the flowchart.
checks.b_evidence_output: The reading order sequence is: 1. Heading, 2. Paragraph (Contact Info), 5. Image (Logo), 6. Image (Flowchart).

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains several explicit numbered lists within the steps of the flowchart (e.g., the 5-item list in 'Execution of License and Usage Agreement' and the 2-item list in 'CJLEADS Trains User Admins'). The output JSON fails to preserve these as semantic list elements, instead capturing the entire diagram as a single image. This results in the loss of the document's structured list data.
checks.b_evidence_source: Numbered list in Step 2 containing items: '1. Identifies & provides Signatory Authority', '2. Identifies internal points of contact', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON contains no elements with 'type': 'list'. The information is exclusively part of an image alt-text summary.

checks.a_analysis: The contact email address 'cjleadshelp@nc.gov' is clearly visible in the source PDF as a text-based contact link. In the output JSON, this entire string is omitted from the contact paragraph. Additionally, the phone number '919-754-6949' is present as plain text but lacks a functional tel: link markup. The complete omission of the email link is a structural defect.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text 'cjleadshelp@nc.gov' is missing from the paragraph content at reading_order 2.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. The document's primary content—a complex flowchart—is presented as a single cohesive image, preserving the spatial relationships between the steps. The heading is correctly positioned at the top. The inclusion of primary contact information as text at the top of the HTML, while it also appears within the graphic, is a standard accessibility practice and does not negatively impact the visual layout or organizational flow.
The source document does not contain any tables; the content is organized as a diagrammatic flowchart with labeled boxes, which is rendered correctly as an image in the HTML.

All meaningful images from the source, including the North Carolina state seal, the CJLEADS logo, and the entire multi-step flowchart diagram, are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output. A descriptive caption/alt-text block is also provided below the diagram to support accessibility.

No body content paragraphs or tables are duplicated. While the document title and primary contact information appear both as searchable text at the top and as part of the rendered flowchart graphic, this is an intentional accessibility design choice to ensure critical information is machine-readable and does not constitute a visual duplication defect.
